home.hero_desc: Powered by Yamu HyperDNS — next-generation DNS infrastructure built for performance, security, and global reach.
home.what_desc: 1.1.8.8 is a free public DNS resolver operated by Yamu Networks. Built on the Hybrid DNS model, we partner with local ISPs worldwide to deploy HyperDNS infrastructure directly inside their networks — delivering faster resolution, stronger security, and better reliability than any remote public DNS.
home.feature_performance_desc: Powered by HyperDNS with up to 20 million QPS cache throughput per device. Smart domain-based traffic splitting ensures hot domains are served from cache with sub-millisecond latency.
home.feature_security_desc: Multi-layered defense against DDoS, amplification attacks, and DNS hijacking. Trusted domain database with real-time learning minimizes false positives while protecting recursive resources.
home.feature_global_desc: 6 strategically placed nodes across North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific. Our Hybrid DNS model brings DNS resolution inside ISP networks, closer to end users than any centralized public DNS.
home.feature_smart_desc: Intelligent cache refresh keeps popular domains always ready. Expired cache activation (RFC 8767) ensures resolution continues even during upstream outages for up to 7 days.
home.feature_protocols_desc: Full support for DNS over UDP, TCP, TLS (DoT), HTTPS (DoH), and QUIC (DoQ). DNSSEC validation ensures response authenticity and integrity.
home.feature_privacy_desc: No logging of personal browsing data. We resolve your queries and forget them. Minimal data retention focused solely on service reliability.
home.network_desc: Strategically deployed nodes ensure low-latency resolution worldwide. We're actively expanding with ISP partners across the United States.
home.expanding: Expanding across the US with local ISP partners
home.tech_desc: Yamu's HyperDNS represents nearly 20 years of carrier-grade DNS experience. Unlike traditional L3 load-balanced DNS architectures, HyperDNS introduces domain-aware traffic splitting — separating hot, normal, and regulated domain traffic for optimal resource allocation.

clients.hero_desc: Configure your devices to use 1.1.8.8 for faster, more secure DNS resolution. We support all modern DNS protocols.
clients.proto_udp_desc: Classic DNS over UDP on port 53. The fastest option with lowest overhead. Works everywhere.
clients.proto_tcp_desc: DNS over TCP on port 53. More reliable for large responses and in restrictive networks.
clients.proto_dot_desc: Encrypted DNS on port 853. Prevents eavesdropping and manipulation of your DNS queries.
clients.proto_doh_desc: DNS over HTTPS on port 443. Blends with web traffic, hard to block, supported by all major browsers.
clients.proto_doq_desc: Next-gen encrypted DNS over QUIC. Combines the security of DoT with lower latency connection setup.
clients.setup_desc: Choose your platform to get started with 1.1.8.8.
windows_steps: Open Settings → Network & Internet → your connection
Click Edit next to DNS server assignment
linux_steps: Edit /etc/resolv.conf or use your network manager
ios_steps: Open Settings → Wi-Fi → tap your network
android_steps: Open Settings → Network & Internet → Private DNS
router_steps: Log into your router admin panel
Find DNS settings (usually under WAN or DHCP)

faq.a: Yes. 1.1.8.8 is completely free for all users. No registration required.
Yes. We fully validate DNSSEC signatures to protect you from forged DNS responses.

faq.q: What is the secondary DNS address?
faq.a: We currently operate a single address: 1.1.8.8. Redundancy is provided by our globally distributed node infrastructure.
For most users, standard UDP works great. If you want encryption, DoH is the most widely supported option. DoQ offers the best combination of security and speed if your client supports it.
DNS resolution is the first step of every internet connection. Faster DNS means faster page loads. With our global nodes and ISP partnerships, 1.1.8.8 resolves queries closer to you.

isps.hero_subtitle: The Hybrid DNS Model — Public DNS Meets Local Infrastructure
isps.hero_desc: 1.1.8.8 pioneers the Hybrid DNS approach: we deploy HyperDNS directly inside your infrastructure, combining the reach and reliability of public DNS with the speed and control of local deployment. A real alternative to Google DNS and Cloudflare.
isps.why_control_desc: Unlike third-party DNS services that route your customers' queries to distant data centers, our partnership model deploys DNS directly in your infrastructure. Your network, your data, your control.
isps.why_performance_desc: HyperDNS's domain-aware architecture separates hot domain traffic (95% of queries) from long-tail domains. Hot domains are served at 100% cache hit rate with <0.1ms latency — something generic DNS can't match.
isps.why_cost_desc: HyperDNS dramatically reduces device count. A node that traditionally needs 47 devices can be served by just 7-8 HyperDNS units. Less hardware, less power, less rack space, less maintenance.
isps.why_security_desc: Domain-based traffic isolation keeps attack traffic away from critical domains. The trusted domain database with real-time learning provides precise DDoS protection with minimal false positives.
isps.why_alternative_desc: Your customers already use Google (8.8.8.8) or Cloudflare (1.1.1.1). Partner with 1.1.8.8 to offer a DNS service that's faster because it's local, more secure because it's on your network, and backed by proven carrier-grade technology.
isps.why_support_desc: Yamu Networks brings nearly 20 years of carrier-grade DNS deployment experience from serving major telecom operators. Full deployment support, monitoring, and ongoing optimization included.
isps.deploy_desc: We scale with your needs. Start small, grow as your customer base demands.
isps.tier1_for: For ISPs serving up to 100K subscribers
tier1_features: Deploy on your existing VM infrastructure
Smart cache refresh and trusted domain learning
All DNS protocols: UDP, TCP, DoT, DoH, DoQ
tier2_features: Dedicated bare metal servers for maximum throughput
Up to 2M QPS cache per node
Domain hash load balancing for recursive efficiency
Elastic scaling — add or remove capacity on demand
tier3_features: Line-rate packet processing, <1ms latency, dual 100G ports
Full HyperDNS U10 rack system (up to 70M QPS)
GRE tunnel support for remote disaster recovery
isps.arch_desc: Traditional DNS uses L3 load balancing that distributes traffic blindly across devices — every device handles every domain. HyperDNS introduces a fundamentally different approach.
isps.arch_old_desc: Every device processes all domains. Redundant cache, wasted resources, and complex operations.
isps.arch_new_desc: Traffic is split by domain type. Hot domains go to high-performance cache servers. Long-tail domains go to recursive-optimized servers.
arch_benefits: 95% of traffic served from dedicated cache at 100% hit rate
Recursive servers handle only 5% of traffic, reducing load dramatically
Attack traffic is isolated from critical domain resolution
Each device role is optimized for its specific workload
Device count reduced by 70-80% vs traditional architecture
isps.cta_desc: Join the 1.1.8.8 network. We're actively looking for ISP partners across the United States and globally.
isps.collab_desc: Hybrid DNS combines the best of both worlds — the technology and global network of a public DNS service with the speed and sovereignty of local ISP infrastructure. We bring the tech, you bring the resources.
collab_we: HyperDNS software suite — battle-tested across major telecom operators
Domain-aware traffic splitting engine and trusted domain database
Full deployment, configuration, and ongoing optimization support
Software updates, security patches, and feature upgrades
Centralized management console and analytics dashboard
collab_you: Virtual machines, bare metal servers, or rack space
Anycast or DNS redirection to route customer queries
Local network knowledge and customer relationships
Operational staff for hardware maintenance (if bare metal)
isps.collab_result: The Hybrid DNS advantage: your customers get a DNS service that's physically inside your network — faster than any remote public DNS, more secure because traffic never leaves your infrastructure, and backed by carrier-grade technology trusted by operators worldwide.
isps.collab_failover: If your local node ever goes down, DNS traffic automatically fails over to our other global nodes or partner ISP nodes. Your customers experience zero downtime — resolution continues seamlessly while you restore your infrastructure. No manual intervention needed.
isps.vs_title: Hybrid DNS vs Remote Public DNS
isps.vs_desc: When your customers use Google DNS (8.8.8.8) or Cloudflare (1.1.1.1), their DNS queries travel to distant data centers. With 1.1.8.8 deployed on your network, resolution happens locally.
isps.vs_metric_latency_them: 20–80ms round trip to remote data center
isps.vs_metric_control_them: Customer queries sent to third-party servers abroad
isps.vs_metric_control_us: All queries resolved within your own network
isps.vs_metric_accuracy_them: CDN routing errors — users get distant content servers
isps.vs_metric_accuracy_us: Accurate ECS/EDNS responses from your local IP ranges
isps.vs_metric_resilience_them: Dependent on external provider uptime and routing
isps.vs_metric_cost_them: Free, but you lose customer data and control
isps.vs_metric_cost_us: Partnership model — shared investment, shared benefit
isps.cases_desc: These aren't hypothetical scenarios. Major DNS outages have taken down entire regions of the internet — affecting millions of users and costing billions in losses. Hybrid DNS ensures your network never depends on a single external provider.
isps.case_1_detail: A massive Mirai botnet DDoS attack hit DNS provider Dyn, taking down Twitter, Netflix, Reddit, Spotify, PayPal and thousands more. Over 14,000 domains lost DNS service. 8% of Dyn's customers left permanently. ISPs relying on Dyn had zero fallback.
isps.case_2_detail: A bad BGP Flowspec rule cascaded across CenturyLink's global network, causing a 5+ hour outage. Amazon, Cloudflare, Discord, Steam, Hulu and hundreds of ISPs lost connectivity. Every network peering with CenturyLink was affected.
isps.case_3_detail: A configuration change caused Cloudflare's 1.1.1.1 public resolver to go down for 62 minutes. Millions of users worldwide lost internet access — especially in regions where ISPs had outsourced DNS entirely to Cloudflare.
isps.case_4_detail: An accidental configuration change in Azure Front Door caused an 8.5-hour DNS outage. Azure Portal, Microsoft 365, Xbox, Minecraft and even the Dutch railway system were affected. A single config error, global impact.
isps.case_5_detail: Cloudflare suffered a global outage affecting ~20% of all web traffic. Services like X, OpenAI, and Anthropic went down. Days later, 1.1.1.1 failed again across the US, Canada, Europe, and South America.
cases_hybrid_points: Local resolution — your DNS runs inside your network, not on someone else's infrastructure thousands of miles away
Automatic failover — if your local node goes down, traffic routes to our other global nodes or partner ISPs seamlessly
7-day expired cache survival (RFC 8767) — even if all upstream servers are unreachable, your customers still resolve cached domains for a full week
No single point of failure — unlike relying on one public DNS provider, Hybrid DNS distributes risk across your local node and our global network
Full control — you own the infrastructure, you control the routing, you see the data. No surprises from third-party configuration changes
isps.steps_desc: From first contact to serving live DNS traffic — here's how simple it is.
isps.step_1_desc: Reach out to us. Tell us about your network, your customer base, and your goals. We'll evaluate the fit and design a deployment plan together.
isps.step_2_desc: We assess your subscriber count, query volume, and traffic patterns to determine the optimal deployment tier — VM, bare metal, or FPGA-accelerated.
isps.step_3_desc: We deploy HyperDNS on your infrastructure and configure domain-aware traffic splitting, caching policies, and monitoring. Full support from our engineering team.
isps.step_4_desc: You announce our Anycast prefix 1.1.8.0/24 via BGP from your network. DNS traffic starts flowing to your local node — your customers are now on Hybrid DNS.
isps.failover_desc: Watch how Hybrid DNS automatically reroutes traffic when a node goes down. Your customers never notice — DNS queries seamlessly shift to the nearest healthy node within milliseconds.

enterprise.hero_subtitle: Enterprise-Grade DNS, DHCP & IP Address Management
enterprise.hero_desc: Yamu SmartDDI delivers turnkey DDI infrastructure with purpose-built appliances, carrier-grade security, and 24/7 managed support. From internal DNS and DHCP to hybrid cloud failover — everything your organization needs, deployed on your network.
enterprise.ddi_title: DDI — The Foundation of Your Network
enterprise.ddi_desc: DDI is the integrated management of DNS, DHCP, and IP Address Management (IPAM). These three services form the backbone of every enterprise network — without them, nothing connects.
enterprise.ddi_dns_desc: Translates names into addresses. Every time an employee opens an app, sends an email, or joins a call, DNS resolves the destination. If DNS is down, your entire network is effectively offline.
ddi_dns_points: Internal resolution for corporate apps and services
Split-horizon for internal vs. external queries
enterprise.ddi_dhcp_desc: Assigns IP addresses to devices automatically. Every laptop, phone, printer, and IoT device that joins your network gets its address from DHCP. Without it, devices can't communicate.
ddi_dhcp_points: Automatic IP assignment for all devices
Reservations for servers and critical devices
Scope management across VLANs and sites
enterprise.ddi_ipam_desc: The single source of truth for your IP space. IPAM tracks every subnet, every allocation, and every device across all sites — giving you visibility into what's on your network and where.
ddi_ipam_points: Centralized view of all IP allocations
Integration with DNS and DHCP for real-time accuracy
enterprise.ddi_together_desc: When DNS, DHCP, and IPAM operate as separate, disconnected systems, errors compound — a DHCP change that DNS doesn't know about causes resolution failures, orphaned IP records create security blind spots, and troubleshooting becomes guesswork. Yamu SmartDDI unifies all three with carrier-grade security, IPv4/IPv6 dual-stack support, device fingerprint identification, and integration with CI/CD and ITSM workflows — so a device getting an IP address automatically gets the right DNS records, and IPAM reflects the change instantly.
enterprise.inc_title: When DDI Fails, Your Business Stops
enterprise.inc_desc: DDI failures don't just affect websites — they take down internal applications, email, VoIP, authentication, and every service your employees depend on. These incidents happened to the world's largest companies.
enterprise.inc_1_detail: A routine maintenance command accidentally disconnected Facebook's entire internal backbone. Internal DNS servers withdrew their BGP routes, making all services — Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, Messenger — completely unreachable worldwide. Engineers couldn't even access buildings because badge systems depended on the downed DNS.
enterprise.inc_2_detail: A faulty CrowdStrike update crashed millions of Windows servers that hosted DNS, DHCP, and Active Directory together. Organizations lost network access, DNS resolution, and authentication simultaneously — because all three services ran on the same infrastructure. Flights grounded, hospitals disrupted, emergency services compromised.
enterprise.inc_3_detail: A misconfiguration linked 1.1.1.1 IP prefixes to a non-production service. When a test location was added, it triggered a global BGP withdrawal of the resolver's prefixes. Millions of businesses using Cloudflare as their sole DNS resolver lost all internet connectivity instantly.
enterprise.inc_4_detail: A race condition in AWS's automated DNS system left an empty DNS record for DynamoDB's regional endpoint. The failure cascaded across 113 AWS services including EC2, Lambda, and SQS. Netflix, Slack, Coinbase, Atlassian, and thousands of businesses went down — 4 million outage reports in 2 hours.
enterprise.inc_5_detail: An oversized Bot Management configuration file crashed Cloudflare's proxy servers globally. DNS queries went unanswered, CDN stopped serving content, and Workers became unresponsive. X, Spotify, and thousands of enterprise services dependent on Cloudflare were knocked offline.
inc_prevent_points: Decoupled infrastructure — DNS, DHCP, and identity services run on dedicated appliances, never sharing failure domains with other systems
On-site control — your DNS runs locally in your server room, not dependent on a third-party cloud provider's configuration changes
Dual hot-spare — two appliances per site with sub-second failover, so a single hardware or software failure never takes down DNS
Hybrid Cloud backup — if both local appliances fail, DNS automatically fails over to our 1.1.8.8 cloud with your exact configuration
No single point of failure — three layers of protection ensure your employees never lose access to DNS, DHCP, or internal resources
enterprise.services_desc: The Yamu SmartDDI platform — purpose-built appliances (SmartDDI-3300/3600/3900 series) with carrier-grade software, designed for enterprises that demand reliability, security, and expert support.
enterprise.svc_dns_public_desc: Enterprise-grade access to the 1.1.8.8 public DNS network. Fast, secure resolution with DoT, DoH, DoQ support and DNSSEC validation. Ideal as a backup resolver or primary for remote workers.
enterprise.svc_ddi_desc: Integrated DNS, DHCP, and IPAM in a single appliance. Automate IP allocation, enforce naming policies, device fingerprint identification, and maintain a real-time view of every device on your network.
enterprise.svc_dhcp_desc: Centralized DHCP management with intelligent IP allocation, lease tracking, conflict detection, and device fingerprinting. Supports scopes, reservations, and dynamic DNS updates across all sites with full IPv4/IPv6 dual-stack.
enterprise.svc_dns_mgmt_desc: Full internal and external DNS management with carrier-grade security — built-in DNS firewall, DDoS protection, anomalous packet filtering, and rate limiting. Split-horizon, policy-based forwarding, and multi-exit intelligent routing from a unified console.
enterprise.svc_managed_desc: Let us handle everything. Subscription-based fully managed SmartDDI — we monitor, maintain, update, and optimize your entire DDI infrastructure around the clock. Integrates with your CI/CD and ITSM workflows.
enterprise.svc_support_desc: Round-the-clock technical support from DDI infrastructure engineers. Incident response, automated fault detection and recovery, configuration assistance, and proactive monitoring — included with every deployment.
enterprise.how_desc: No need to source hardware, build software, or hire DNS specialists. We provide the complete stack, installed on your network and managed by our team.
we_provide: SmartDDI appliances (3300/3600/3900 series), deployed on-site in your server room
Carrier-grade SmartDDI software with kernel-level security acceleration
Full DDI platform: DNS + DHCP + IPAM with IPv4/IPv6 dual-stack
DNS firewall, DDoS protection, and zero-trust compatible policy engine
Unified management portal with API, CI/CD integration, and audit logging
24/7 monitoring, automated fault recovery, and SLA-backed support
you_get: Enterprise DDI that runs locally on your network — no cloud dependency
Carrier-grade DNS security: firewall, DDoS mitigation, threat detection
DDI failover protection via our Hybrid Cloud 1.1.8.8 network
Multi-site management from a single dashboard with full API access
Zero DDI expertise required from your team — we handle everything
Predictable subscription pricing — no surprise hardware costs
enterprise.adv_desc: Enterprise DNS can't afford downtime. Our architecture ensures your DNS infrastructure stays online no matter what.
enterprise.adv_failover_desc: Your local DNS configuration is continuously synced to our 1.1.8.8 Hybrid Cloud. If your on-premises appliance goes down, the cloud seamlessly serves the same configuration — same zones, same records, zero disruption.
adv_failover_points: Local config automatically synced to cloud in real-time
Cloud serves identical DNS responses during local failure
Automatic failback when local appliance recovers
enterprise.adv_hotspare_desc: Every deployment includes dual appliances running in active-active or active-passive configuration. Hardware failure in one unit triggers instant, sub-second failover to the standby — your users never notice.
adv_hotspare_points: Two appliances per site — always ready
Sub-second failover on hardware or software failure
Combined with cloud failover: three layers of protection
enterprise.adv_remote_desc: Manage DNS and DHCP across all your offices from a single unified portal. Deploy new sites in minutes, push configuration changes globally, and maintain full visibility into every location.
adv_remote_points: Unified dashboard for all sites — HQ, branches, remote offices
Easy deployment: ship appliance, plug in, auto-configures
Site-to-site configuration sync and policy inheritance
Centralized reporting and audit logging across all locations
enterprise.dia_desc: Watch the three-tier failover in action: on-site primary, hot-spare standby, and cloud backup. Your DNS stays online through any failure scenario.
enterprise.msite_desc: Employees roam between campuses with the same IP address. Off-site, DoH certificates with device-level identifiers keep them securely connected to corporate resources.
enterprise.msite_feat_roam_desc: When an employee moves between campuses or branch offices, their device keeps the same IP address. DHCP reservations sync across all sites via the unified SmartDDI portal — no reconnection, no VPN reconfiguration, no disruption.
enterprise.msite_feat_identity_desc: Each device is issued a DoH certificate with a unique employee identifier. DNS queries are authenticated at the device level — the system knows who is querying, enforces per-user policies, and blocks unauthorized devices from corporate DNS resources.
enterprise.msite_feat_offsite_desc: Employees working from home, traveling, or at client sites install a DoH certificate on their device. DNS queries route to our 1.1.8.8 DoH gateway, which authenticates the device and provides the same internal DNS resolution as if they were in the office.
enterprise.cost_desc: Our managed SmartDDI service eliminates hidden costs — no DDI specialists to hire, no hardware to maintain, no downtime to recover from.
cost_points: No need to hire or train in-house DNS infrastructure engineers — our team manages everything remotely
Hardware included in subscription — no upfront capital expenditure for appliances, spares, or replacements
Proactive monitoring catches issues before they cause outages — reducing incident costs and business disruption
Multi-site management from one portal eliminates redundant tools, licenses, and per-site support contracts
Predictable monthly pricing — no surprise costs from emergency hardware replacements or consultant fees
enterprise.cta_desc: Talk to our team about deploying Yamu SmartDDI on your network. Custom solutions for organizations of any size.
